question: H-domain-ref
claim: REFERENCE (C5-A): SerializationId (ids.rs:121-147) hash(kind_tag ++ 0x00 ++ canonical_key)->fixed hex prefix; 0x00+kind-tag namespaces so identical bytes across kinds never collide; fact key = triple ONLY (excludes mint-id AND rule_version) for cross-rebuild identical render (prefix-cache contract). Render-order vs score-order separation (retriever.rs:452-465; tiebreak by serialization-id then content, NEVER mint-id).

question: H-domain-ref
claim: CRITICAL PORTABILITY GOTCHA: NaN-safe total float ordering (fusion.rs:135 b.score.total_cmp(&a.score).then(a.node.cmp(&b.node))). JS (a,b)=>b-a is NOT a total order — NaN poisons the sort, -0/+0 collapse. EVERY determinism candidate (C5-A/B, Q4-C1, det-context-order-global, DL-merge-tiebreak) MUST hand-write a total comparator (explicit NaN handling, -0 vs +0) + content-derived id tiebreak before claiming deterministic order. Single most important determinism portability finding.

question: H-domain-ref
claim: REFERENCE: canonical JSON before hashing (signing.rs:182-200, recursively sort object keys at every depth, arrays positional) so map-construction order can't perturb hashed bytes — the invariant C4-B/C5-A rely on. Dual-class identity (ids.rs:18-71, UUIDv7 mint + UUIDv8 content-hash, shared parse) ports as UUIDv7/ULID + sha256-hex.
